message:
chore(gitignore): ignore agent commit-helper detritus

Parallel agents periodically drop one-off "task scripts" and captured stdout
into the working tree as they drive cargo-check/git-commit wrappers
(e.g. do_task.sh, final_commit_script.out, nohup.out, scripts/do_*.py,
scripts/*_helper.py). These are ephemeral by design — they're orchestration
glue that writes its real output to /home/ubuntu/.gemini/tmp or to log files
under /tmp, not artifacts that belong in source control.

Add narrow patterns covering the actual filenames seen in this repo plus
their obvious siblings:

  nohup.out                    universal nohup capture
  /do_*.sh                     root-level "do_<task>.sh" runners
  /*.out                       root-level captured stdout (e.g. *.out from `> file`)
  /final_commit_*              root-level final_commit_script.* siblings
  scripts/do_*.py              do_finalize_raid.py and friends
  scripts/*_helper.py          *_helper.py commit/finalize wrappers
  scripts/final_*.py           final_commit_helper.py and siblings

Patterns are deliberately scoped (root-anchored or scripts/-prefixed) so they
don't accidentally suppress real .out fixtures or .py utilities elsewhere in
the tree. Verified with `git check-ignore -v` against all five untracked
files currently in the working tree.

Sits next to the existing `fix_*.py` line which already documents the same
agent-generated-script class of detritus.

files.patch:
@@ -140,6 +140,17 @@ third_party/
 # One-off migration/fix scripts (agent-generated)
 fix_*.py
 
+# Agent commit-helper detritus (one-off task scripts and captured output).
+# These are produced by parallel agents driving `git commit`/`cargo check`
+# wrappers and writing logs back to /tmp; they have no place in the repo.
+nohup.out
+/do_*.sh
+/*.out
+/final_commit_*
+scripts/do_*.py
+scripts/*_helper.py
+scripts/final_*.py
+
 # NTM session state
 .ntm/
